name: Hello Graph: Generate Lego Pirate Ship Cover

data.prompt:
Generate a book cover for a pirate ship built from lego
Have a big bad ship on the main cover
The ship must be built from lego bricks

Have no title or subtitle.
data.output: High-resolution, professional-grade book cover image. A colossal, formidable pirate ship stands prominently in the center of the frame, meticulously detailed and unequivocally built entirely from LEGO bricks. The distinctive LEGO aesthetic is paramount, with every visible stud, the unmistakable texture of plastic building blocks, and intricate brickwork clearly rendered. This vessel exudes immense power and a menacing, 'bad' aura. It boasts numerous towering masts with complex, LEGO-built rigging, billowing, tattered sails (constructed from LEGO fabric pieces or cleverly arranged plates), multiple cannons protruding from its sides, and a tattered flag, perhaps a skull and crossbones, waving defiantly from its peak. The ship churns through a rough, turbulent sea with dynamic, realistically rendered waves and foam. Above, the sky is dramatically lit, either a brewing storm with dark, heavy clouds and flashes of distant lightning, or a fiery, intense sunset casting long, ominous shadows across the scene. The overall style is a hyper-realistic, cinematic render of a highly detailed LEGO model, showcasing sharp focus, exquisite textures, and professional lighting to emphasize its imposing scale and formidable presence. Absolutely no text, titles, subtitles, author names, or any other typographic elements are to be included in the image.

data.prompt:
Welcome to minivagent!

This basic flow demonstrates how to turn a simple text idea into a high-quality image.

1. The Text Node enhances your rough prompt.
2. The Image Generation Node creates the visual result.
